what_it_does: Renders a static HTML composition titled "The Full Flow Walks the Roles" with five small phone mockups connected in sequence: open, choose, work, review, and close. Inline CSS adjusts the compact phone layout, and inline JavaScript populates two row lists.
observations: Loads Google fonts plus shared `/surface/styles/piece.css` and `/surface/styles/phone.css`.
Uses a `.view` container with decorative background points, a title block, a horizontal phone sequence, a mark, and a footer element.
Shows five phone states labeled `open · 85`, `choose · 50`, `work · 35`, `review · 40`, and `close · 80`.
Connector joints between phones use `sock`, `dash`, and `plug` spans.
The first and last phones use background classes and hatch overlays; middle phones use structured headers, rows, plates, bands, and actions.
Inline scripts fill `#r1` with three seam choices and `#r2` with two checked record rows.
standouts: The file presents the app flow as a single left-to-right visual arc across five phone screens.
Each screen has a role label with a numeric value beneath the phone mockup.
The layout combines ornamental workshop language with mobile UI components and progress-like connectors.
conventions: Uses shared design tokens through CSS custom properties such as `--dna-space-*`, `--dna-type-*`, `--gold`, and `--charcoal`.
Keeps most structural styling class-based while using inline styles for the final checkmark emblem.
Uses compact modifier classes such as `.sm` to resize existing phone components.
Populates repeated row markup with small inline JavaScript arrays and template literals.
concerns: Several final-screen styles are inline, which makes that screen less reusable than the class-based portions.
The JavaScript assumes `#r1` and `#r2` exist without guards.
The checkmark is a literal glyph inside styled markup rather than a reusable icon or component.
notes: A single-page visual storyboard of five connected mobile app states for a workshop-themed full-flow presentation.
questions: What do the numeric role values such as 85, 50, 35, 40, and 80 represent?
Is `/surface/runtime/organisms.js` required for behavior in this file, or only for shared runtime setup?
Are the workshop terms literal product concepts or presentation metaphors?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and labels state a full flow across roles, and the file directly renders that five-screen role sequence.
intention_signals: It seems intended to communicate an end-to-end app experience as a compact visual sequence, with each phone representing a role or phase in the flow.
novelty: The file treats a multi-screen app flow as a connected physical procession, using socket-and-plug joints and workshop language rather than ordinary app-screen thumbnails.
contribution: Contributes a compact five-state mobile flow composition, role labels with numeric annotations, connector-joint sequencing, and craft-oriented screen copy that a fused system could keep as a flow-story substrate.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS/JS page showing five connected small phone mockups for an app flow from workshop opening through choosing, working, reviewing, and closing.
intention_for_embedding: Designed to visualize the full app journey as one connected sequence of roles and states using shared phone UI styling and workshop-themed language.
